title: Review Suspense and error boundary placement
description: Verify async UI is wrapped by a Suspense fallback and an error boundary at the right granularity so failures and loading stay localized.
body: Steps: 1. Map each async/lazy/data-fetching subtree in the changed UI. 2. Confirm a Suspense boundary wraps it with a fallback that reserves layout space. 3. Confirm an error boundary wraps it so a thrown error or rejected resource shows recovery UI instead of unmounting the whole tree. 4. Check the boundary is at a granularity that does not collapse the whole page on one widget failure. 5. Trigger a forced error and a slow resource to observe behavior. Rollback point: before moving boundaries higher in the tree. Validation: a forced rejection renders the error fallback and a throttled load renders the Suspense fallback, evidenced in the browser, not just unit tests.
